english_name: The First Epistle of Paul the Apostle to Timothy

verses.latin_vulgate: Paulus Apostolus Jesu Christi secundum imperium Dei Salvatoris nostri, et Christi Jesu spei nostræ,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παῦλος ἀπόστολος ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ κατ᾽ ἐπιταγὴν θεοῦ σωτῆρος ἡμῶν καὶ κυριόυ ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ τῆς ἐλπίδος ἡμῶν
verses.king_james: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the commandment of God our Saviour, and Lord Jesus Christ, *which is* our hope;

verses.latin_vulgate: Timotheo dilecto filio in fide. Gratia, misericordia, et pax a Deo Patre, et Christo Jesu Domino nostro.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τιμοθέῳ γνησίῳ τέκνῳ ἐν πίστει χάρις ἔλεος εἰρήνη ἀπὸ θεοῦ πατρὸς ἡμῶν καὶ χριστοῦ ἰησοῦ τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν
verses.king_james: Unto Timothy, *my* own son in the faith: Grace, mercy, *and* peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut rogavi te ut remaneres Ephesi cum irem in Macedoniam, ut denuntiares quibusdam ne aliter docerent,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καθὼς παρεκάλεσά σε προσμεῖναι ἐν ἐφέσῳ πορευόμενος εἰς μακεδονίαν ἵνα παραγγείλῃς τισὶν μὴ ἑτεροδιδασκαλεῖν
verses.king_james: As I besought thee to abide still at Ephesus, when I went into Macedonia, that thou mightest charge some that they teach no other doctrine,

verses.latin_vulgate: neque intenderent fabulis, et genealogiis interminatis: quæ quæstiones præstant magis quam ædificationem Dei, quæ est in fide.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μηδὲ προσέχειν μύθοις καὶ γενεαλογίαις ἀπεράντοις αἵτινες ζητήσεις παρέχουσιν μᾶλλον ἢ οἰκονομίαν θεοῦ τὴν ἐν πίστει
verses.king_james: Neither give heed to fables and endless genealogies, which minister questions, rather than godly edifying which is in faith: *so do*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Finis autem præcepti est caritas de corde puro, et conscientia bona, et fide non ficta.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸ δὲ τέλος τῆς παραγγελίας ἐστὶν ἀγάπη ἐκ καθαρᾶς καρδίας καὶ συνειδήσεως ἀγαθῆς καὶ πίστεως ἀνυποκρίτου
verses.king_james: Now the end of the commandment is charity out of a pure heart, and *of* a good conscience, and *of* faith unfeigned:

verses.latin_vulgate: A quibus quidam aberrantes, conversi sunt in vaniloquium,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὧν τινες ἀστοχήσαντες ἐξετράπησαν εἰς ματαιολογίαν
verses.king_james: From which some having swerved have turned aside unto vain jangling;

verses.latin_vulgate: volentes esse legis doctores, non intelligentes neque quæ loquuntur, neque de quibus affirmant.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: θέλοντες εἶναι νομοδιδάσκαλοι μὴ νοοῦντες μήτε ἃ λέγουσιν μήτε περὶ τίνων διαβεβαιοῦνται
verses.king_james: Desiring to be teachers of the law; understanding neither what they say, nor whereof they affirm.

verses.latin_vulgate: Scimus autem quia bona est lex si quis ea legitime utatur:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἴδαμεν δὲ ὅτι καλὸς ὁ νόμος ἐάν τις αὐτῷ νομίμως χρῆται
verses.king_james: But we know that the law *is* good, if a man use it lawfully;

verses.latin_vulgate: sciens hoc quia lex justo non est posita, sed injustis, et non subditis, impiis, et peccatoribus, sceleratis, et contaminatis, parricidis, et matricidis, homicidis,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰδὼς τοῦτο ὅτι δικαίῳ νόμος οὐ κεῖται ἀνόμοις δὲ καὶ ἀνυποτάκτοις ἀσεβέσιν καὶ ἁμαρτωλοῖς ἀνοσίοις καὶ βεβήλοις πατραλῴαις καὶ μητραλῴαις ἀνδροφόνοις
verses.king_james: Knowing this, that the law is not made for a righteous man, but for the lawless and disobedient, for the ungodly and for sinners, for unholy and profane, for murderers of fathers and murderers of mothers, for manslayers,

verses.latin_vulgate: fornicariis, masculorum concubitoribus, plagiariis, mendacibus, et perjuris, et si quid aliud sanæ doctrinæ adversatur,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πόρνοις ἀρσενοκοίταις ἀνδραποδισταῖς ψεύσταις ἐπιόρκοις καὶ εἴ τι ἕτερον τῇ ὑγιαινούσῃ διδασκαλίᾳ ἀντίκειται
verses.king_james: For whoremongers, for them that defile themselves with mankind, for menstealers, for liars, for perjured persons, and if there be any other thing that is contrary to sound doctrine;

verses.latin_vulgate: quæ est secundum Evangelium gloriæ beati Dei, quod creditum est mihi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κατὰ τὸ εὐαγγέλιον τῆς δόξης τοῦ μακαρίου θεοῦ ὃ ἐπιστεύθην ἐγώ
verses.king_james: According to the glorious gospel of the blessed God, which was committed to my trust.

verses.latin_vulgate: Gratias ago ei, qui me confortavit, Christo Jesu Domino nostro, quia fidelem me existimavit, ponens in ministerio:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ χάριν ἔχω τῷ ἐνδυναμώσαντί με χριστῷ ἰησοῦ τῷ κυρίῳ ἡμῶν ὅτι πιστόν με ἡγήσατο θέμενος εἰς διακονίαν
verses.king_james: And I thank Christ Jesus our Lord, who hath enabled me, for that he counted me faithful, putting me into the ministry;

verses.latin_vulgate: qui prius blasphemus fui, et persecutor, et contumeliosus: sed misericordiam Dei consecutus sum, quia ignorans feci in incredulitate.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸν πρότερον ὄντα βλάσφημον καὶ διώκτην καὶ ὑβριστήν ἀλλ᾽ ἠλεήθην ὅτι ἀγνοῶν ἐποίησα ἐν ἀπιστίᾳ
verses.king_james: Who was before a blasphemer, and a persecutor, and injurious: but I obtained mercy, because I did *it* ignorantly in unbelief.

verses.latin_vulgate: Superabundavit autem gratia Domini nostri cum fide, et dilectione, quæ est in Christo Jesu.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑπερεπλεόνασεν δὲ ἡ χάρις τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν μετὰ πίστεως καὶ ἀγάπης τῆς ἐν χριστῷ ἰησοῦ
verses.king_james: And the grace of our Lord was exceeding abundant with faith and love which is in Christ Jesus.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fidelis sermo, et omni acceptione dignus: quod Christus Jesus venit in hunc mundum peccatores salvos facere, quorum primus ego sum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πιστὸς ὁ λόγος καὶ πάσης ἀποδοχῆς ἄξιος ὅτι χριστὸς ἰησοῦς ἦλθεν εἰς τὸν κόσμον ἁμαρτωλοὺς σῶσαι ὧν πρῶτός εἰμι ἐγώ
verses.king_james: This *is* a faithful saying, and worthy of all acceptation, that Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners; of whom I am chief.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed ideo misericordiam consecutus sum: ut in me primo ostenderet Christus Jesus omnem patientiam ad informationem eorum, qui credituri sunt illi, in vitam æternam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλὰ διὰ τοῦτο ἠλεήθην ἵνα ἐν ἐμοὶ πρώτῳ ἐνδείξηται ἰησοῦς χριστὸς τὴν πᾶσαν μακροθυμίαν πρὸς ὑποτύπωσιν τῶν μελλόντων πιστεύειν ἐπ᾽ αὐτῷ εἰς ζωὴν αἰώνιον
verses.king_james: Howbeit for this cause I obtained mercy, that in me first Jesus Christ might shew forth all longsuffering, for a pattern to them which should hereafter believe on him to life everlasting.

verses.latin_vulgate: Regi autem sæculorum immortali, invisibili, soli Deo honor et gloria in sæcula sæculorum. Amen.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τῷ δὲ βασιλεῖ τῶν αἰώνων ἀφθάρτῳ ἀοράτῳ μόνῳ σοφῶ θεῷ τιμὴ καὶ δόξα εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας τῶν αἰώνων ἀμήν
verses.king_james: Now unto the King eternal, immortal, invisible, the only wise God, *be* honour and glory for ever and ever. Amen.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hoc præceptum commendo tibi, fili Timothee, secundum præcedentes in te prophetias, ut milites in illis bonam militiam,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ταύτην τὴν παραγγελίαν παρατίθεμαί σοι τέκνον τιμόθεε κατὰ τὰς προαγούσας ἐπὶ σὲ προφητείας ἵνα στρατεύῃ ἐν αὐταῖς τὴν καλὴν στρατείαν
verses.king_james: This charge I commit unto thee, son Timothy, according to the prophecies which went before on thee, that thou by them mightest war a good warfare;

verses.latin_vulgate: habens fidem, et bonam conscientiam, quam quidam repellentes, circa fidem naufragaverunt:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔχων πίστιν καὶ ἀγαθὴν συνείδησιν ἥν τινες ἀπωσάμενοι περὶ τὴν πίστιν ἐναυάγησαν
verses.king_james: Holding faith, and a good conscience; which some having put away concerning faith have made shipwreck:

verses.latin_vulgate: ex quibus est Hymenæus, et Alexander: quos tradidi Satanæ, ut discant non blasphemare.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὧν ἐστιν ὑμέναιος καὶ ἀλέξανδρος οὓς παρέδωκα τῷ σατανᾷ ἵνα παιδευθῶσιν μὴ βλασφημεῖν
verses.king_james: Of whom is Hymenaeus and Alexander; whom I have delivered unto Satan, that they may learn not to blaspheme.
